title: 4 Week Beginner Kettlebell Workout For Muscle Growth
summary: Burn fat and gain muscle with this 4-week beginner's to kettlebell training workout that's just as effective as traditional weightlifting programs.
author: Roger \u201cRock\u201d Lockridge, Senior Writer

notes.label: Day 1
notes.note: Perform each movement for 2 sets of 15 reps with 30 seconds rest between sets after your normal workout.

notes.label: Day 2
notes.note: Perform each movement for 3 sets of 15 reps with 30 seconds rest between sets after your normal workout. Take a day off from kettlebells after this workout.

notes.label: Day 3
notes.note: Perform each movement for 3 sets of 20 reps with 30 seconds rest between sets after your normal workout.

notes.label: Day 4
notes.note: Perform each exercise for 30 seconds in giant set fashion. Perform three giant sets with 30 seconds rest between each giant set. This is your last kettlebell workout for the week.

notes.label: Day 1
notes.note: Upper Body: Basic Russian Swing, Push Press, Snatch, One Arm Row – each exercise for 3 sets of 20 reps with 30 seconds rest between sets.

notes.label: Day 2
notes.note: Lower Body and Core: Goblet Squat, Lunge, Russian Twist, Windmill - each exercise for 3 sets of 20 reps with 30 seconds rest between sets. Take a day off from kettlebell training.

notes.label: Day 3
notes.note: Upper Body Circuit: Basic Russian Swing, Push Press, Snatch, One Arm Row – three giant sets of each exercise for 45 seconds with 30 seconds rest between giant sets.

notes.label: Day 4
notes.note: Lower Body Circuit: Goblet Squat, Lunge, Russian Twist, Windmill - three giant sets of each exercise for 45 seconds with 30 seconds rest between giant sets.

notes.label: Day 1
notes.note: Each movement for three sets of 10 reps. Rest 30 seconds between sets.

notes.label: Day 2
notes.note: Each movement for three sets of 20 reps. Rest 30 seconds between sets. Take a day off from kettlebell training.

notes.label: Day 3
notes.note: Each movement for three giant sets of 10 reps each with 30 seconds rest between giant sets.

notes.label: Day 4
notes.note: Each movement for three giant sets of 20 reps each with 30 seconds rest between giant sets.

notes.label: Day 1
notes.note: Each exercise performed consecutively for 30 seconds followed by a 60 second rest period. Repeat for three circuits.

notes.label: Day 2
notes.note: Each exercise performed consecutively for 45 seconds followed by a 60 second rest period. Repeat for three circuits.

notes.label: Day 3
notes.note: Each exercise performed consecutively for 60 seconds followed by a 45 second rest period. Repeat for three circuits.

notes.label: Day 4
notes.note: Each exercise performed consecutively for 60 seconds followed by a 30 second rest period. Repeat for three circuits.

title: Big Arms Fast: 4 Week Specialization Workout
summary: This four week bicep and tricep building specialization routine from transformation expert Justin Woltering is designed to help you bring up your lagging arms, and fast.
author: Justin Woltering

exercises.exercise: Tricep Dips (As many sets as it takes to get 50 total reps with your body weight.)
EZ Bar Cheat Curls (Make sure your biceps do most of the work!)
Incline Dumbbell Curls (1 rest-pause set for 20 – 30 total reps. Use strict form!)
Rope Push-Downs (1 rest-pause set for 30 – 40 total rep.s)
Super-Set (Use the same weight for each exercise, and aim to hit failure between 10 and 20 reps.)
Reverse Barbell Curls (Cock your wrists back at the top of each rep to  make your forearms do the work.)
Weighted Tricep Dips (Add extra weight if necessary.)
Incline Skull Crushers (Aim for 12-15 reps for the first set, as many as you can get for the second set.)
Preacher EZ Bar Curls (Same protocol as the incline skull crushers.)
Close-Grip Bench Press (try to use the same weight for all sets.)
Close-Grip Chin-Ups (with your body weight, stopping a rep before failure on each set.)
Pin-Press (In the power rack: set up so you have about a 12” range of motion, use a close grip, and come to dead stop between reps. try to use the same weight for all sets.)
Incline Curls (With the same weight; try to hit 15 – 20 reps on the first set.)
Close-Grip Bench Press (Try to use the same weight for all sets.)
Close-Grip Chin-Ups (Try to use the same added weight for all sets.)
Weighted Tricep Dips (Work up to a top set of 5 reps. Rest, reduce the weight by 50%, and do as many reps as you can. Rest, and do a third set with just body weight for as many reps as you can.)
EZ Bar Curl (1 rest-pause set for 20-30 total reps.)
